ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general kebab food product class; no specific vegan chicken tikka kebab class exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM covers meat substitute products broadly; no specific tikka kebab plant-based variant exists. Closest parent code used.

main_ingredients: plant-based chicken substitute (soy or wheat gluten)
tikka spice blend (cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, chilli powder)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab is approximately 150g, equivalent to one skewer of marinated plant-based chicken pieces served at a hawker stall or casual dining outlet in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab (cr_id G00010866) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat was not reported in the HPB record and has been estimated at approximately 5.5g/100g based on the known fat composition of soy/wheat-gluten plant-based products cooked in vegetable oil, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for comparable plant-based meat products (USDA SR Legacy). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and moisture data are not available in the HPB record or USDA FoodData Central for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on mixed plant-based protein dish with spiced marinade
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous plant-based protein dishes with minimal carbohydrate content (5.24g/100g) and high protein/fat ratio, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable low-carbohydrate plant-based meat preparations. Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: (35 × 5.24 × 1.5) / 100 = 2.75; rounded to 1.8 per 100g equivalent basis. GL per serving (150g) = (35 × 7.86) / 100 ≈ 2.75.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g — critically exceeds the 600mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab due to its critically high sodium content of 1080mg/100g, which represents 54% of the 2000mg daily sodium limit in a single 100g portion; request a reduced-salt preparation or limit to a very small tasting portion only.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24g/100g) and low GI (~35) are favourable, but high fat (26.4g/100g) and high sodium (1080mg/100g) require portion control
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate content (5.24g/100g) and estimated low GI of approximately 35 make Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab suitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes, but the high fat content (26.4g/100g) and critically elevated sodium (1080mg/100g) necessitate strict portion control and avoidance of pairing with refined carbohydrate accompaniments such as naan or white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g and estimated saturated fat ~5.5g/100g elevate cardiovascular risk; total fat 26.4g/100g is also high
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should exercise significant caution with Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab due to its critically high sodium content (1080mg/100g), estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.5g/100g, and total fat of 26.4g/100g, all of which contribute to elevated blood pressure and atherogenic risk; consumption should be infrequent, in small portions, and paired with low-sodium, high-fibre accompaniments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 1080mg/100g exceeds 400mg/100g RA gate; estimated saturated fat ~5.5g/100g triggers pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab is unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to sodium at 1080mg/100g (far exceeding the 400mg/100g RA threshold) and estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.5g/100g, both of which promote TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity; request a significantly reduced-salt, lower-fat preparation and monitor individual tolerance to nightshade-derived spices such as red chilli powder.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegan Chicken Tikka Kebab is a plant-based adaptation of the classic South Asian chicken tikka kebab, widely available in Singapore's hawker centres, food courts, and casual dining outlets. The dish replaces traditional chicken with a plant-based protein substitute — typically made from soy protein isolate or wheat gluten (seitan) — marinated in a tikka spice blend comprising cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, chilli powder, ginger, garlic, and a dairy-free yogurt alternative such as coconut or soy yogurt. The marinated pieces are skewered and grilled or chargrilled, producing a smoky, charred exterior with a tender, spiced interior. The flavour profile is bold, aromatic, and mildly spicy, with earthy spice notes from turmeric and cumin balanced by the tang of lemon juice. Nutritionally, the dish provides 302 kcal per 100g, with a high fat content of 26.4g/100g (estimated saturated fat ~5.5g/100g), moderate protein at 10.7g/100g, and very low carbohydrate at 5.24g/100g, making it low-GI (estimated GI ~35). However, sodium is critically high at 1080mg/100g, which is a significant concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. The dish is fully vegan and dairy-free but may contain soy and gluten allergens. It is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management in small portions due to its low carbohydrate and low GI profile, but the high sodium and fat content require careful portion control. The cholesterol value of 85mg/100g reported in the HPB source is notable and may reflect the specific plant-based product formulation used.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010866) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous plant-based meat product data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology for low-carbohydrate plant-based protein dishes. Overall confidence is high for macronutrients and secondary nutrients from HPB; medium for saturated fat and GI estimates.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific vegan chicken tikka kebab preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the HPB source for this specific preparation. 3. Saturated fat estimated at 5.5g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for comparable plant-based soy/wheat-gluten meat products cooked in vegetable oil; this is an adapted estimate, not a direct HPB measurement. 4. Cholesterol at 85mg/100g is as reported by HPB; this value is unusual for a vegan product and may reflect the specific plant-based formulation or a data anomaly in the source record — flagged for follow-up review. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Halal status is not confirmed as the specific plant-based protein substitute brand and slaughter-free certification status are unknown. 7. The food_subgroup has been updated from 'Chicken-based dishes' to 'Plant-based meat dishes' to accurately reflect the vegan nature of this product.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full RESULT JSON from HPB input. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Added saturated_fat_g estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous data. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Added all three required health_context conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease). Populated ontology with defensible FoodOn and FoodEx2 approximate matches. Documented all -1 fields and confirmed-zero dietary_fibre_g in notes. Verified aliases consistency between aliases_by_language and flat aliases array.
